text: ABSTRACT The purpose of educational evaluation is to discover the relative strengths and weaknesses of educational programs and to form a basis for informed planning and sustainable development. This study has aimed at the identification of the concepts, purposes, principles and phases of educational program evaluation, the facilitators and obstacles of the evaluation process of the educational programs and making recommendations accordingly. According to the study method, literature review was used in the study and the relevant studies and theories were brought together and summarized in a way to evaluate the educational program effectiveness and to improve the educational quality. Results showed that, educational evaluations should be based on objectivity and carried out in line with the goals set for educational programs. It is also important to have phases such as planning, implementation and controlling in a systematic manner for educational evaluation. In addition, the supportive governmental and institutional regulations constitute the major facilitating factors, whereas obstacles are mostly financial and expert-based. For this reason, it is necessary to create an evaluation-friendly environment in institutions and to increase the participation of stakeholders including parents and society towards transforming the assessment findings into substantial improvements in the national education system. Keywords: Evaluation, Educational Evaluation, Facilitators and Obstacles of Evaluation Process.
